PEP: 8
Title: Style Guide for Python Code
Author: Guido van Rossum, Barry Warsaw, Alyssa Coghlan
Status: Active
Type: Process
Created: 05-Jul-2001
Post-History: 05-Jul-2001, 01-Aug-2013

Abstract
========

This document gives coding conventions for the Python code comprising
the standard library in the main Python distribution.

Motivation
==========

Readability counts. This PEP assumes that a shared style convention
reduces cognitive load for multi-author codebases. We also assume
most tooling can be configured to follow these rules automatically.

This proposal requires ongoing maintenance as the language evolves.

Rationale
=========

Consistency within a project is more important than the exact rules
here. The plan presupposes that automated formatters will eventually
catch up and make hand-editing unnecessary.

When to use multi-line imports depends on reader context, not on
a hard mechanical rule.
